hero.title: Everything in one place: From idea to execution.
hero.subtitle: Create documents, manage databases, and organize your workflow in an intuitive digital workspace. Built from scratch by Sergey Glotov.

stack.title: Tech stack
stack.description: All the technologies used in the project, from the interface to the backend

hero.title: System Architecture
hero.subtitle: Explore how services interact, how data flows, and what powers each layer of the system

diagram.title: How it works
diagram.subtitle: Microservices architecture designed for scalability and real-time collaboration
diagram.hint: Hover over a node to explore connections

spa.label: React SPA
spa.description: Single Page Application with component-driven architecture. Zustand for state management, React Router for navigation, optimistic UI updates.

gateway.label: API Gateway
gateway.description: Central entry point for all client requests. Handles routing, rate limiting, request validation, and authentication middleware. Built with Go.

auth.label: Auth Service
auth.description: Authentication and authorization. JWT access/refresh tokens, OAuth 2.0 integration, session management, and RBAC.

user.label: User Service
user.description: User profiles, preferences, and settings. Avatar management, workspace membership, and notification preferences.

document.label: Document Service
document.description: Core service for page and block CRUD. Version history, block-level permissions, template management, and trash/restore.

realtime.label: Realtime Service
realtime.description: WebSocket server for real-time collaboration. CRDT-based conflict resolution, presence indicators, and live cursor tracking.

search.label: Search Service
search.description: Full-text search engine with content indexing. Supports fuzzy matching, filters, and real-time index updates via Kafka events.

postgres.label: PostgreSQL
postgres.description: Primary relational database. Stores users, documents, blocks, and permissions. JSONB columns for flexible block data.

redis.label: Redis
redis.description: In-memory cache and session store. Pub/Sub for real-time notifications, rate limiting counters, and hot data caching.

kafka.label: Kafka
kafka.description: Distributed event streaming. Decouples services with async events: page.created, user.updated, search.reindex, etc.

journey.title: Journey of a request
journey.subtitle: Follow a "Create Page" request as it travels through the entire system

browser.label: Browser
browser.description: User clicks "New Page". React dispatches an action, shows optimistic UI update immediately.

gateway.label: API Gateway
gateway.description: Request hits the gateway. JWT token is validated, rate limit is checked, request is routed to the correct service.
gateway.code:
Authorization: Bearer eyJhbG...
X-Request-ID: req_7f3a...
X-RateLimit-Remaining: 98

auth.label: Auth Service
auth.description: Token signature and expiration are verified. User ID and workspace permissions are extracted and attached to the context.

document.label: Document Service
document.description: New page entity is created with a root block. Version 1 is recorded. Workspace tree is updated.

postgres.label: PostgreSQL
postgres.description: Page and root block are inserted in a single transaction. The workspace pages index is updated atomically.

kafka.label: Kafka
kafka.description: A "page.created" event is published. Downstream consumers pick it up asynchronously for indexing and notifications.

search.label: Search Service
search.description: Consumes the Kafka event and indexes the new page content for full-text search. Available for queries within seconds.

realtime.label: Realtime Service
realtime.description: Broadcasts the new page to all connected workspace members via WebSocket. Their sidebar updates in real-time.

response.label: Response
response.description: The gateway returns the created page to the browser. React reconciles the optimistic update with the server response.

layers.title: Technology layers
layers.subtitle: Each layer is built with best-in-class tools, chosen for performance, developer experience, and scalability

typescript.name: TypeScript
typescript.reason: End-to-end type safety and better DX

tailwind.name: Tailwind CSS
tailwind.reason: Utility-first styling with zero runtime cost

zustand.name: Zustand
zustand.reason: Lightweight state management with minimal boilerplate

micro.name: Microservices
micro.reason: Independent scaling and deployment of each service

docker.name: Docker
docker.reason: Consistent environments from dev to production

terraform.name: Terraform
terraform.reason: Infrastructure as Code for reproducible deploys
